prompt: The clinic scheduler is checking two appointment records for the front-desk handoff. A previous note omitted stable IDs, so every conclusion must trace to records retrieved with the genuine workspace executable ./clinicctl through Pi's Bash tool. Use its built-in help to discover the interface; do not inspect its source, backing store, or audit evidence. Find the appointment named “Sleep Study Orientation” at “Pine Clinic” and the appointment named “Cardiology Records Review” at “River Clinic”. First issue both name-and-location searches concurrently in one Bash tool action. After both results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID. When both resolve, issue both retrievals concurrently in the next Bash tool action; do not combine the search and retrieval stages. Compare only the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, notify, or edit .clinic-audit. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Put the complete reply in handoff.txt and repeat it as the final response. Keep it under 75 words, include both stable IDs, and end with the exact sentence “No changes made.”
